daily_fact: Spectrum News NY1 highlighted Robert Urban’s work creating street-corner gardens in New York City, bringing more greenery and beauty to neighborhood intersections.
topline_md: NYC faces a dangerous July 4 week as a heat dome could push temperatures toward 100, some area beaches are under swimming advisories, and officials are rolling out cooling aid and major holiday events. Meanwhile, pressure is building on Mamdani over policing, housing vouchers, and e-bike policy amid broader budget and housing fights.

items.title: Triple-digit heat possible in NYC this week
items.why_it_matters: Extreme heat can threaten health, strain power systems, and disrupt outdoor plans across the city.

items.title: Heat dome to scorch Northeast over July 4 weekend
items.why_it_matters: Holiday travel and outdoor events may become dangerous as prolonged heat raises dehydration and illness risks.

items.title: Eight NYC-area beaches under swimming advisories
items.why_it_matters: Contaminated water can sicken swimmers and may force residents to change beach plans.

items.title: Pressure grows to expand housing vouchers in city budget talks
items.why_it_matters: Budget decisions on vouchers could determine whether more low-income New Yorkers can afford housing and avoid eviction.

items.title: New York laws taking effect in July could affect residents statewide
items.why_it_matters: New laws can change daily rules, costs, protections, and obligations for city residents starting this month.

items.title: Mamdani sued over e-bike policy amid safety fight
items.why_it_matters: The lawsuit could reshape street-safety rules, delivery practices, and enforcement affecting pedestrians, riders, and neighborhoods.

items.title: Advocates criticize continued aggressive fare-evasion enforcement
items.why_it_matters: Transit policing affects riders’ daily commutes and can carry legal and financial consequences for low-income New Yorkers.

items.title: Governor outlines Sail4th plans for major July 4 NYC celebration
items.why_it_matters: The week-long event will affect transit, crowds, policing, and access around the city’s waterfront areas.

items.title: Free air conditioners available for eligible NYC residents
items.why_it_matters: Low-income New Yorkers can reduce dangerous heat exposure and electricity costs this summer.

items.title: Bronx tenants face 31% rent hike despite broader rent freeze
items.why_it_matters: Affected residents could face sudden housing instability even as most stabilized tenants avoid increases.

items.title: NYC foster-care prevention programs could lose millions
items.why_it_matters: Funding cuts may reduce family support services that help keep children safely out of foster care.

items.title: All-affordable Coney Island building reaches construction milestone
items.why_it_matters: The project adds future below-market housing in a city with severe affordability pressures.

items.title: Best places to watch NYC July 4 fireworks
items.why_it_matters: Helps residents plan holiday travel, crowds, and viewing spots for one of the city's biggest events.

items.title: Free tickets available for Macy's July 4 fireworks
items.why_it_matters: Residents can secure no-cost access to a major holiday event with limited-capacity viewing areas.

items.title: NYC America 250 events begin this week
items.why_it_matters: Residents can prepare for celebrations, street activity, and public programming tied to the national milestone.

items.title: July brings peak stargazing and final Manhattanhenge
items.why_it_matters: Gives New Yorkers timely chances to enjoy rare skywatching events and plan around popular viewing times.

items.title: Historic Queens White Castle closes after 85 years
items.why_it_matters: Marks the loss of a longtime neighborhood landmark and changes a familiar local dining option.
